recipes.title: Shakshuka
recipes.description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce — a weekend classic.
steps: Heat the olive oil in a wide pan and soften the onion and pepper, about 7 minutes.
Add the garlic, cumin and paprika and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the tomatoes, season, and simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
Make wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each one.
Cover and cook 6-8 minutes until the whites are set but yolks are still soft.
Scatter with herbs and serve straight from the pan with bread.
recipes.notes: Add crumbled feta in the last few minutes for a richer version.

recipes.title: Tahini Cookies
ingredients: 100 g soft butter or coconut oil
steps: Mix all ingredients into a smooth, slightly crumbly dough.
Roll into walnut-sized balls and place on a lined tray.
Press an almond into the top of each ball.
Bake 12 minutes — they should stay pale. Cool completely before moving.

recipes.title: Red Lentil Soup
recipes.description: A warming one-pot soup that comes together from pantry staples.
steps: Sauté the onion and carrots in olive oil until soft, about 8 minutes.
Stir in the garlic, tomato paste and cumin and cook 1 minute.
Add the lentils and stock, bring to a boil, then simmer 25 minutes.
Blend partially for a creamy-but-chunky texture.
Finish with lemon juice, season, and serve.
recipes.notes: Freezes well for up to 3 months.
